batch_metadata.notes: Lowest conflict batch to date (7%). Post-surgery recovery period. Phone-blocking arc quiet — no 8:30pm enforcement events. 'Not to your house' restriction from B-004 does not appear, consistent with surgery recovery being complete.

unflagged_notable.reason_noted: Melissa grants Eli an evening pickup but layers in conditions: home by 8 (ROW-1471), shower needed and early morning (ROW-1472), 15 minutes reading (ROW-1476), dinner (ROW-1478). These are routine co-parenting logistics with legitimate bases. Not flagged as RF-02 because the conditions are facially reasonable household routine requests, not arbitrary gatekeeping attachments. No indication conditions were used to block or deter the visit.

Melissa tells Eli 'I don't know why you felt the need to tell Olivia you were talking to lawyers. She doesn't need involved in that.' (Feb 15, car accident context.) Melissa is policing what Eli discusses with Olivia about his own legal situation. Near-boundary RF-11 (Inappropriate Child Involvement — Melissa managing what topics Eli may raise with his child). Has some legitimacy (legal proceedings are complex) but also controlling element. Not flagging as RF-02 since this is about communication content rather than access restriction.

Melissa relays Olivia's demand: 'Olivia said anytime I call you I expect you to answer!' Shows Olivia actively wanting contact with Eli — analytically positive data point. Eli missing calls appears to be a two-way pattern. Noting for completeness.

Melissa explains Olivia went to get stuff from car, got interrupted, got mad, and 'doesn't want to call you back' as a result of the frustration. This is Melissa providing context for why Olivia didn't call — a normal explanation of child behavior. Distinct from the CA-RF02-02 child-preference-relay pattern because: (1) Melissa's explanation is specific and situational, not a blanket preference statement, (2) no DND infrastructure in play, (3) Olivia's mood is independently verifiable through context. Not flagged.

peripheral_flags_summary.note: Car accident dispute (Feb 15): Melissa accuses Eli of staging injuries for insurance fraud ('I can't believe you're suing that poor lady. You just said you guys were fine. Then as soon as you found out she had insurance all of a sudden you're hurt.' ROW-1716). 'I don't want you teaching her how to be a scammer.' (ROW-1734). Emotional manipulation — accusation of dishonesty designed to shame and delegitimize Eli's legitimate legal response to the accident. Eli did not admit to fraud; he explained his reasoning (ROW-1749).

'Maybe that wreck did fuck you up. You hit your head? Maybe you do need to get checked out.' — Derogatory/mocking language in response to Eli raising a factual point about Melissa's statements to Olivia. Mild intimidation through ridicule.

arc_state_snapshot.arc_quiet_note: Second consecutive quiet batch for ARC-RF02-001. Possible explanations: (1) Melissa's announcement of strict enforcement (ROW-861) deterred Eli from calling after 8:30; (2) the post-surgery period involved different communication patterns; (3) Eli may have adjusted call timing. Watch for resumption in B-006.
arc_state_snapshot.candidate_watching: ARC-RF02-004 candidate ('not to your house' restriction) does not activate this batch — pre-surgery restriction appears resolved. Candidate remains dormant unless pattern resumes.
